Today, we are working on the dev branch, and we will need to update the logic for AI reviewed papers to include a new endpoint focused on revised papers  here is the documentation of the API in html https://www.dropbox.[REDACTED]-API-DocsRevise.html?rlkey=5aaw24seekcwuppc8jivzv105&dl=0 and in PDF form https://www.dropbox.[REDACTED]-API-REVISE.pdf?rlkey=5e5kp7gva2kdhomf9mu06uw8p&dl=0 . The goal of today's update is to complete the AI review part of the submission process which we have right now in the dev branch. The pipeline now needs to include an option to resubmit a paper using the /revise method of the API. This means we need to track the {sessionID} of a paper when we first submit it, so that we can then submit a revised manuscript that is an updated version of the same paper, but addressing the comments of the original submission. The revise endpoint will score each comment in a a four point scale and also includes a desk reject option in case the resubmission is not good enough. Uploads a revised PDF and an author response, then evaluates how well the original review comments were addressed. This is a synchronous endpoint — it blocks until the evaluation is complete and returns the scored results.

The review session must be completed before a revision can be submitted.

Each comment from the original review is scored on a 1–4 scale:

1 — Ignored: No mention in author response, no changes in paper.
2 — Weakly acknowledged: Author mentions the comment but no meaningful paper changes.
3 — Well acknowledged: Meaningful paper changes that substantially address the concern.
4 — Fully addressed: Thorough changes that completely resolve the concern.
The endpoint may also return a desk rejection (deskReject: true) if the submission is non-substantive, contains manipulation attempts, or includes unprofessional language. We will test this by submitting a new version of a paper addressing the comments. We need to generate a resubmission screen that focuses on submitting a new version of the paper, as well as a document with point by point response to the reviewers. The new version of the paper will be submitted to the revise endpoint for evaluation. Please let me know if you have any questions about this before you proceed. This is an important refactor and we need to make sure we do not mess up anything we have working at the moment.

We want to make the process as auditable and transparent as possible. The endorser cannot be the same as in the last time. For 4, yes, the author can revise again, up to three times. There is one important detail, what is send back for revision as a response to the API is not the author response letter, but the revised manuscript file.
